task.title: Implement Build Contract v2 and closure briefs
task.summary: Make compiler builds high-signal transition contracts by adding mandatory feedback diff rows, implementation closure briefs, explicit consumes/produces edges, schema/lint enforcement, and compact user-facing closure output.

goal.outcome: New builds are compact, structured, and trustworthy transition points that preserve user intent and prove task closure through all layers.
acceptance: `codewiki_build` accepts and persists feedback diff-table rows with current state, desired state, rationale, affected layers, risk, and user action.
New accepted feedback builds require approved diff rows and expose them as canonical intent contract data.

`codewiki_build kind=implementation` accepts and persists a compact closure_brief with user intent, implemented changes, layers updated, acceptance evidence, checks, non-goals preserved, and remaining risks.
New builds expose explicit `consumes` and `produces` fields; graph reconciliation prefers those edges over legacy inference.
Lint/schema/tests flag missing v2 required fields for new builds while treating historical builds as legacy cold artifacts.
Task-close or implementation summary can show the closure brief so users can trust their intent was implemented through knowledge, roadmap, code/tests, checks, and validation.
non_goals: Do not migrate every historical build immediately.
Do not implement the interactive diff-table UI in this task.
Do not make pending unapproved diff rows canonical truth.
verification: Create smoke feedback/documentation/implementation builds using v2 fields and confirm graph edges/closure brief output.

summary: Implemented Build Contract v2: feedback builds now require approved diff_table rows, implementation builds require closure_brief, build writers emit explicit consumes/produces DAG fields, graph reconciliation consumes those edges, and lint/tests enforce v2 contracts while leaving historical builds as legacy cold artifacts.

evidence: Added smoke tests for v2 success paths and rejection of missing mandatory feedback diff rows / implementation closure brief.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added v2 build inputs, writer normalization, DAG fields, graph edges, and v2 lint enforcement.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added smoke tests for v2 success paths and rejection of missing mandatory feedback diff rows / implementation closure brief.
code_change_evidence: Added v2 build inputs, writer normalization, DAG fields, graph edges, and v2 lint enforcement.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_build accepts and persists feedback diff-table rows.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: schemas.ts exposes diff_table/approved_diff_rows; builds.ts normalizes rows, derives accepted decisions from approved rows, and smoke tests assert persisted rows.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: New accepted feedback builds require approved diff rows.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: writeFeedbackBuild rejects accepted feedback builds without approved diff_table rows; tests assert rejection.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Implementation builds accept and persist closure_brief.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: schemas.ts/types.ts expose closure_brief; builds.ts requires closure_brief for accepted implementation builds; smoke tests assert persisted closure brief.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: New builds expose explicit consumes and produces fields.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: feedback/documentation/implementation writers emit consumes/produces; graph.ts adds DAG edges and reconciliation indexes explicit consumed refs.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Lint/schema/tests flag missing v2 required fields while treating historical builds as legacy.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: lint.ts enforces v2 only when schema_version >= 2; historical builds without schema_version remain legacy; tests cover missing diff rows and missing closure brief.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Task-close or implementation summary can show closure brief.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: implementation_build.closure_brief contains compact user-facing proof with intent, layer changes, acceptance evidence, checks, preserved non-goals, and risks.

closure_brief.user_intent: Make builds high-signal transition points so user intent is explicitly accepted through feedback diff rows and closure proves implementation succeeded across knowledge, roadmap, code/tests, checks, and validation.
implemented_changes: Feedback builds persist approved diff_table rows and reject accepted builds without approved rows.
Implementation builds persist mandatory closure_brief and reject accepted builds without it.
Build writers emit explicit consumes/produces fields for DAG handoffs.
Graph reconciliation and graph edges consume explicit build DAG fields before legacy inference.
Lint enforces v2-only required fields; tests cover success and missing-field failures.
acceptance_evidence: Smoke tests assert persisted diff rows, consumes/produces, closure brief, and mandatory-field rejection.
non_goals_preserved: Historical builds were not migrated wholesale.
Interactive diff-table UI was not implemented.
Pending unapproved diff rows were not made canonical truth.
remaining_risks: Pi reload is required for live tool schema exposure.

risks: Live Pi tool schema needs reload before codewiki_build accepts new diff_table/closure_brief fields through the API.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: feat(builds): enforce v2 handoff contracts
commit.body:
Implemented Build Contract v2: feedback builds now require approved diff_table rows, implementation builds require closure_brief, build writers emit explicit consumes/produces DAG fields, graph reconciliation consumes those edges, and lint/tests enforce v2 contracts while leaving historical builds as legacy cold artifacts.

Checks: npx tsc --noEmit, node scripts/check-architecture.mjs, npm test
Validation: .codewiki/validation/2026-05-11-task-055-implementation-pass.json
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
